claim: No Stable, release, conformance, package-parity, browser-file-picker, or recorded manual assistive-technology claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: A real input type=file is labelled by the visible action and optionally described by visible text.
semantics.naming: A non-empty visible label is mandatory and the description is connected through aria-describedby.
semantics.roleOverride: The file-input role and owned selection attributes cannot be replaced by consumer roles.

interaction.keyboard: Tab focuses the native file control; platform Enter or Space activation opens the browser picker.
interaction.pointer: The complete visible label activates the native control without a synthetic click handler.
interaction.touch: The visible label and overlaid input preserve at least a 44 CSS pixel target and may request supported camera capture.
interaction.speech: The visible action text is the native control name for voice targeting.
interaction.switchAccess: One native control is exposed without a duplicate hidden button.

formBehavior: name and form remain native file-input attributes; multiple, accept, capture, directory selection, reset, disabled omission, and browser security policy remain browser-owned. One internal sibling helper may synchronize a bounded real-File list for composites; it validates target type, multiplicity, count, and assignment, clears before failure, and dispatches no synthetic input or change event.

states.synchronization: Visible disabled styling, native disabled state, focus, selected FileList, callback snapshot, and form data derive from the same input.

preferences.enhancedContrast: Uses boundary geometry and visible text rather than color alone.
preferences.forcedColors: Uses ButtonFace, ButtonText, and GrayText with the native input retained.
preferences.reducedMotion: No selection or focus behavior depends on motion.

responsive.overflowPolicy: Label and description wrap without clipping; the native target follows the label box.

internationalization.serialization: File bytes and names remain browser File objects and are never serialized by the component.

consumerResponsibilities: Explain accepted type, count, size, privacy, and upload behavior before opening the picker.
Treat accept as picker guidance and validate files again before preview or transport.
Do not read or upload selected files without an explicit product action and clear privacy copy.
Provide a non-camera alternative whenever capture is requested.
limitations: Source-present-unreleased records do not include packed-consumer, picker/device, package/source parity, or manual assistive-technology evidence.
Directory and camera capture support is browser and platform dependent; applications must retain an ordinary file-selection alternative.
